name: Slowroll Golf & Social
about: Slowroll Golf & Social is a golf simulator venue located in Skippack Village, offering a private space to play over 1,000 world-class courses year-round. The facility operates on a flat hourly rate model, where one fee covers the entire group regardless of size, and features a TruGolf Apogee launch monitor running E6 software. It is designed for social play, practice, and small events, with a pro shop available for merchandise.
why_golfers_like_it: Over 1,000 world-class courses available to play
Flat hourly rate pricing, one fee for your entire group
BYOB and BYOF policy to make your own night
TruGolf Apogee launch monitor with E6 software for accurate simulation
Private, relaxed atmosphere perfect for social golf

food_and_drink.notes: BYOF (Bring Your Own Food) is allowed; guests can bring takeout from nearby restaurants. A small selection of snacks and soft drinks is also available for purchase.
BYOB (Bring Your Own Beer) is allowed and encouraged.

simulator_hardware.notes: Launch monitor used for ball and club tracking

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The context does not specify whether clubs are provided, so it is recommended to bring your own.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, Slowroll is BYOB (Bring Your Own Beer) and BYOF (Bring Your Own Food). Guests can bring takeout from nearby restaurants or bring their own drinks. A small selection of snacks and soft drinks is also available for purchase.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The rule of thumb is to plan for about 1 hour per player for a full 18-hole round.

faq.question: Is Slowroll kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The context does not explicitly state that Slowroll is kid-friendly. It is primarily marketed as a social spot for adults, focusing on golf, drinks, and events.
